author: @martynharveythepoet5114
text: Soapbox moment appreciated Rob! This is exactly what's wrong with our whole economy. Billions invested in short-lived fads.. which generate loads of money for the investors (and "fuel the economy")... ie: rip off the consumers... and leave all of us paying for it until our grandchildren... do the same thing. Hopefully, this is one of the major benefits of this channel... to promote the importance and environmentally-important aspects of everything we do... to provide more awareness of the shallowness (and limitations) of our consumer society...and to suggest ways we can find a way forward. It may seem like "preaching to the converted" but... it has to be done so - go for it! ... if it makes 1 out of a 1000 think a bit harder - "Job done"! :-)

author: @jamescampbell7823
text: Strong magnets? Yeah, I'd say that's an accurate statement although a bit understated lol. I have about four of these wheels in various stages of parts salvaging and I think there's around 30 or so neodymium magnets in each one they're glued to the rim obviously because they're in north south facing it would just pile up in one knot if they weren't glued down, but I found an ice pick or something very similar in between them each one at a time peels them up, you just have to be sure to slip something like a knife underneath it just as soon as you get a crack or else it's going to flip and stick to the one next to it and then... well you'll find out soon enough because it's going to happen regardless. So if there's  somebody that has never messed around with neodymium magnets this should be quite interesting for them,it's an eye opener that's for sure,  they're a whole other level, honestly nothing else in mechanics makes me laugh as much as rare earth  magnets because to me they're often like wayward little children always up to no good and wanting to do exactly what you don't want them to do right when you're not looking.

author: @Traderhood
text: I got four of those for $25 each. That’s eight 350W motors for $100 bucks. You can use them for mid-motor ebike conversions.

author: @wilhallman2890
text: I don't think you fully understand how useful these really are. 3 of my kids have one each and they are constantly zinging around the house on them. It's kind of incredible really how fast they whip around, sometimes even squatting down, spinning around... and the things go for a very long time. But, I'm still waiting for them to break (if they ever do, they're very well built) to turn them into windmills.
